name: Customer pinch-zooms the line-items table on a small-screen public quote
description: On a 360px-wide Android phone, the public-quote line items table can't render at desktop density. The page uses a viewport meta that ALLOWS user-scalable=yes (no maximum-scale lock-out) so the customer can pinch to zoom the table for detail. Header and totals stay readable at default zoom too — pinch-zoom is a backstop, not the only way to read.

persona.name: Asha Patel
persona.role: Reviewing the quote on her older Android
persona.context: Holding the phone close — small text is hard to read without zooming.

preconditions: Public quote URL reachable; quote has 5+ line items with mixed quantities and units

steps.expect: Header (contractor + business + scope) is legible at default zoom; line items table is readable but compact

steps.action: Pinch-zooms in on the line items table
steps.expect: Browser allows zoom up to ~3×; no script blocks the gesture; layout reflows aren't required
Page returns cleanly to default zoom; no orphan scroll position

evaluationCriteria.check: Viewport meta is 'width=device-width, initial-scale=1' WITHOUT maximum-scale=1 or user-scalable=no
Customer never feels trapped in a too-small layout
Table cells use overflow-wrap so long descriptions don't horizontal-scroll the whole page on default zoom
WCAG 1.4.4 (resize text) is satisfied at 200% browser zoom; pinch-zoom is allowed per WCAG 1.4.10
